description: Bridges the two regime taxonomies. Thesis regimes are narrative-rich (7 categories from docs/macro_thesis_v3.md). Framework regimes are stable scoring categories (5 categories from config/trading_framework.json macro_regimes). Each thesis regime maps to one or more framework regimes with a confidence weight; the brain uses the framework regime for scoring and the thesis regime for narrative display.

war_escalation.rationale: Resource insecurity and energy shocks favor real assets (commodity-led), but risk appetite typically contracts (risk-off). Defense leadership is a sub-case of risk-off rotation.

inflation_shock.rationale: Rising inflation expectations and narrowing policy flexibility align directly with commodity-led inflation; sustained fiscal stress can layer in debasement dynamics.

growth_scare.rationale: Demand slowdown and rising earnings sensitivity drive defensive positioning. Until policy relief is priced, leadership rotates inconsistently (chop).

dovish_liquidity_wave.rationale: Cleanest mapping. Soft policy expectations + returning risk appetite = risk-on expansion.

broad_panic.rationale: First phase is unambiguously risk-off. Recovery phase often passes through chop before regime clarifies.

commodity_expansion.rationale: Multi-quarter supply + demand acceleration = commodity-led leadership. If fiscal/sovereign stress accompanies, monetary debasement dynamics layer in.

usage_notes: regime_classifier agent outputs both: (a) thesis regime for narrative display, (b) framework regime for scoring
When thesis regime maps to two framework regimes with non-trivial secondary weight, brain may blend scoring modifiers proportionally
If user updates thesis to vN+1 with new narrative regimes, this file must be updated alongside; framework taxonomy is stable
